member.name: Mr Peter Bedford
member.synopsis: Mr Peter Bedford is the Conservative MP for Mid Leicestershire , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Children's Commissioner for England: Operating Costs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what the total running costs of the office of the Children's Commissioner for England was for the last full year in which data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Office of the Children’s Commissioner for England is an independent non-departmental public body. The Children’s Commissioner’s Annual Report and Accounts for 2024/25 are available here: https://www.childrenscommissioner.gov.uk/corporate-governance/an...

writtenQuestions.heading: Information Commissioner's Office: Operating Costs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what the total running costs of the office of the Information Commissioner was for the last full year in which data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The total running costs of the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) are set out in its Annual Reports, which are available on ICO’s website: https://ico.org.uk/about-the-ico/our-information/annual-reports/.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Charity Commission: Operating Costs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what the total running costs of the office of the Charity Commission for England & Wales was for the last full year in which data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In the financial year 2025-2026, the total running costs of the Charity Commission for England & Wales’ offices, including lease costs, was £1,672,633.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Judicial Appointments Commission
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what the total running costs of the office of the Judicial Appointments Commission was for the last full year in which data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Judicial Appointments Commission (JAC) publishes a report annually which includes information related to running costs. The latest published report (2024-25) can be found on the JAC website: JAC Annual Report and Accounts 2024 to 2025. The data for th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Service Commission: Costs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what the total running costs of the office of the Civil Service Commission was for the last full year in which data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Civil Service Commission annual report and accounts 2024 to 2025 can be found here: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/civil-service-commission-annual-report-and-accounts-2024-to-2025/civil-service-commission-annual-report-and-accounts-2024-to...

writtenQuestions.heading: Victims' Commissioner
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what the total running costs of the office of the Victims' Commissioner for England and Wales was for the last full year in which data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The running costs for the Office of the Victims’ Commissioner for the last full year 2024/2025 was £859,698 of a total budget of £887,000. A breakdown of this is outlined on page 55 of the recently published annual report: The Office of the Victims Commis...

writtenQuestions.heading: Equality and Human Rights Commission: Costs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, what the total running costs of the office of the Equality & Human Rights Commission was for the last full year in which data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The EHRC is an independent arm’s length body that is operationally independent of the Government. Their annual budget is set by the Cabinet Office. The Equality Act 2006 sets out the legal duty for the Secretary of State to lay before Parliament a copy of...

writtenQuestions.heading: Small Business Commissioner: Operating Costs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what the total running costs of the office of the Small Business Commissioner was for the last full year in which data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Office of the Small Business Commissioner publishes its audited Annual Report and Accounts on an annual basis, setting out its performance and expenditure. These are laid before Parliament pursuant to the Enterprise Act 2016. The most recent accounts ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Patient Safety Commissioner: Operating Costs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what the total running costs of the office of the Patient Safety Commissioner for England was for the last full year in which data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The most recent total for running costs recorded against the Patient Safety Commissioner is for the financial year 2025/26, at £353,053. This figure is currently unaudited and remains subject to the National Audit Office’s review of the Department’s 2025/...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Animal Experiments: Dogs and Primates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, with reference to the Answer on 11 June 2026 to Question 7009, whether her Department holds any baseline figures on the number of dogs and non-human primates used in dedicated pharmacokinetic studies
writtenQuestions.answerText: An approach to measure progress in reaching the target to reduce such use by at least 35% by 2030 is being developed with DSIT colleagues and will be included in a strategy delivery update published later in 2026.The Home Office does not presently aggrega...

writtenQuestions.heading: Animal Experiments: Dogs and Primates

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office does not publish a separate aggregated category for the number of dogs and non-human primates used specifically in dedicated pharmacokinetic studies for human medicines for publication purposes conducted under the Animals (Scientific Proce...

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Political Impartiality
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what recent discussions her department has had with technology companies on the political neutrality of AI applications.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The details of Ministerial meetings with external stakeholders are published in quarterly transparency returns.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Gambling: Risk Assessment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what estimate she has made of the percentage of people who were identified under the Financial Risk Assessments (a) 2025 pilot and (b) further pilot analysis phase.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is for the Gambling Commission to decide whether to implement Financial Risk Assessments. DCMS supports the policy intent behind Financial Risk Assessments, and has had regular engagement with the Gambling Commission on this issue.The pilot has provide...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: British International Investment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what recent assessment she has made of the effectiveness of the risk and reward profile for investment decisions of the British International Investment plc.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) receives comprehensive access to information on the performance of British International Investment across a range of areas, including its financial return target and development impact target. Furth...

writtenQuestions.heading: Trade Promotion
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what recent assessment he has made of the effectiveness of British Trade Envoys in securing economic growth opportunities for the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Trade Envoy programme was established in 2012 as a network of parliamentarians to provide international support to the UK Government’s strategy to drive economic growth.DBT Trade Envoys play a valuable role in supporting the UK’s trade and investment ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Gift Aid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of gift aid contribution rates on levels of charitable giving.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Gift Aid enables charities to claim a basic rate top-up on eligible donations. HMRC does not hold information centrally on the proportion of Gift Aid contributions by donors’ marginal rates of Income Tax for the 2024–25 and 2025–26 financial years. This i...
